findings.evidence: PR lines 57-61 define only `externalTensorCoact`; Mathlib has the needed tensor coalgebra infrastructure at `mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:45` (`instCoalgebraStruct`) and `:130` (`instCoalgebra`). The roadmap target explicitly calls for comodule tensor products at `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/ReductiveGroups/README.md:126`.
findings.fix: Import `Mathlib.RingTheory.Coalgebra.TensorProduct`, prove the coassociativity and counit laws for this map, and provide the tensor-product comodule instance first; keep `externalTensorCoact`/formula lemmas as the coaction API derived from that instance.
findings.issue: `externalTensorCoact` exposes only the raw map `M ⊗ N → (M ⊗ N) ⊗ (C ⊗ D)` instead of the natural `Comodule R (C ⊗[R] D) (M ⊗[R] N)` structure.

summary: The API stops at a candidate linear map even though Mathlib already has the tensor-product coalgebra needed to state the natural comodule tensor product. This is under-generalized for the roadmap item it targets.
